title: Substack vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack vs Omnisend. Substack excels for writers monetizing newsletters; Omnisend for ecommerce email + SMS marketing.
h1: Substack vs Omnisend: Which Email Marketing Platform Wins?
intro: Substack and Omnisend serve fundamentally different email marketing needs. Substack is built for writers and creators to monetize subscriber relationships, while Omnisend is purpose-built for ecommerce businesses managing multi-channel campaigns. Choosing between them depends entirely on your business model and primary use case.
choose_a_if: You're a writer or creator looking to monetize your newsletter directly from subscribers
You want unlimited subscribers on a free plan with no upgrade pressure
You prefer simplicity and focus on content distribution over complex marketing automation
choose_b_if: You run an ecommerce business and need email + SMS marketing together
You need advanced automation workflows and pre-built templates for online sales
You want omnichannel capabilities including push notifications and product recommendations

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—they serve different purposes. Substack wins for writers monetizing content directly, while Omnisend excels for ecommerce businesses. Your choice depends on whether you're building a newsletter business or running an online store.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Omnisend?
faq.a: Substack is completely free unless you enable paid subscriptions (taking 30% revenue share). Omnisend is free for up to 250 subscribers, then starts at $16/month. For large subscriber bases, Substack's unlimited free tier is more cost-effective.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow subscriber list exports via CSV. However, they're designed for different use cases, so switching makes sense only if your business model shifts from ecommerce to direct creator monetization. Substack lacks the SMS and automation features most ecommerce businesses rely on.
